nodes.label: AI as informational reference for specialized technical issues
nodes.label_negative: AI as unreliable fabricator of nonexistent settings
nodes.grounding_span: I use AI to find information about setting up and troubleshooting phones, SIP issues, and VoIP concepts

nodes.label: AI accuracy in providing specific technical details
nodes.label_negative: AI hallucination and fabrication of settings
nodes.grounding_span: it makes up that setting...pretends like it's there and wastes my time

nodes.label: AI as conversational troubleshooting partner
nodes.grounding_span: It's conversational. I'll provide the error and then guide the AI through how it initially appeared

nodes.label: AI knowledge depth in specialized telecom domain
nodes.label_negative: AI knowledge limited to general manuals and wiki articles
nodes.grounding_span: AI is currently weaker than me on these topics and rarely teaches me anything new if ever

nodes.label: Efficient problem resolution

nodes.label: Reliable and accurate information
nodes.grounding_span: it makes up that setting...wastes my time

nodes.label: Professional expertise and autonomy
nodes.grounding_span: AI is currently weaker than me on these topics and rarely teaches me anything new if ever

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI for obscure technical issues
nodes.grounding_span: If I'm asking AI then it's not within the internal documentation or company dialogue history, and it'd be too obscure for a teammate to know

nodes.label: Frustration with AI hallucination
nodes.grounding_span: Frustrated is when I ask it for information on setting something up in a phone and it makes up that setting

nodes.label: Skepticism about AI's future improvement in specialized domain
nodes.grounding_span: I don't foresee it knowing anything more than can be found in phone manuals and wiki articles

nodes.label: Conversational iterative probing — guides AI through context step-by-step
nodes.grounding_span: I'll provide the error and then guide the AI through how it initially appeared and what I'm attempting with clients to provide context

nodes.label: Verification-demanding — asks for proof and pushes back on fabrications
nodes.grounding_span: I ask it for the actual setting name and directory, and to provide proof of where this exists
